segments.speaker: AMBER
segments.en: Hello William.
segments.zh: 你好威廉，我是安珀——你说过如果我想了解更多关于你提到的那个职业中介的信息可以打电话给你。现在方便吗？

segments.speaker: AMBER
segments.en: This is Amber – you said to phone if I wanted to get more information about the job agency you mentioned.

segments.speaker: AMBER
segments.en: Is now a good time?

segments.speaker: WILLIAM
segments.en: Oh, hi Amber.
segments.zh: 哦，嗨安珀。方便。那个中介叫Bankside，在道克兰区——我可以告诉你地址——东区497号。

segments.speaker: WILLIAM
segments.en: Yes.

segments.speaker: WILLIAM
segments.en: Fine.

segments.speaker: WILLIAM
segments.en: So the agency I was talking about is called Bankside – they're based in Docklands – I can tell you the address now – 497 Eastside.

segments.speaker: AMBER
segments.en: OK, thanks.
segments.zh: 好的，谢谢。那有没有什么特定的人我应该去找？

segments.speaker: AMBER
segments.en: So is there anyone in particular I should speak to there?

segments.speaker: WILLIAM
segments.en: The agent I always deal with is called Becky Jamieson.
segments.zh: 我一直打交道的代理人叫贝基·贾米森。

segments.speaker: AMBER
segments.en: Let me write that down – Becky ...
segments.zh: 我记一下——贝基……

segments.speaker: WILLIAM
segments.en: Jamieson J-A-M-I-E-S-O-N.
segments.zh: 贾米森，J-A-M-I-E-S-O-N。

segments.speaker: AMBER
segments.en: Do you have her direct line?
segments.zh: 你有她的直线电话吗？

segments.speaker: WILLIAM
segments.en: Yes, it's in my contacts somewhere – right, here we are: 078 double 6, 510 triple 3.
segments.zh: 有，在我通讯录里——找到了：078双6，510三3。我要是你的话，会等到下午再打给她——她上午总是很忙，忙着填补临时空缺。她非常乐于助人也很友好，所以我觉得联系她做个非正式谈话肯定值得。

segments.speaker: WILLIAM
segments.en: I wouldn't call her until the afternoon if I were you – she's always really busy in the morning trying to fill last-minute vacancies.

segments.speaker: WILLIAM
segments.en: She's really helpful and friendly so I'm sure it would be worth getting in touch with her for an informal chat.

segments.speaker: AMBER
segments.en: It's mainly clerical and admin jobs they deal with, isn't it?
segments.zh: 他们主要处理的是文职和行政工作，对吧？

segments.speaker: WILLIAM
segments.en: That's right.
segments.zh: 没错。我知道你最终想找一份媒体行业的全职工作——但贝基主要招聘金融行业的临时员工——这会让你的简历好看，而且通常薪水也更高。

segments.speaker: WILLIAM
segments.en: I know you're hoping to find a full-time job in the media eventually – but Becky mostly recruits temporary staff for the finance sector – which will look good on your CV – and generally pays better too.

segments.speaker: AMBER
segments.en: Yeah – I'm just a bit worried because I don't have much office experience.
segments.zh: 是啊——我只是有点担心，因为我没有太多办公室经验。

segments.speaker: WILLIAM
segments.en: I wouldn't worry.
segments.zh: 我不会担心的。他们可能会先让你做接待员之类的工作。所以对于这类工作来说，重要的不是商业技能或熟悉很多不同的电脑系统——沟通才是真正重要的——所以你在这方面没问题。而且你会在工作中很快学会办公室技能。没那么复杂。

segments.speaker: WILLIAM
segments.en: They'll probably start you as a receptionist, or something like that.

segments.speaker: WILLIAM
segments.en: So what's important for that kind of job isn't so much having business skills or knowing lots of different computer systems – it's communication that really matters – so you'd be fine there.

segments.speaker: WILLIAM
segments.en: And you'll pick up office skills really quickly on the job.

segments.speaker: WILLIAM
segments.en: It's not that complicated.

segments.speaker: AMBER
segments.en: OK good.
segments.zh: 好的。那么人们通常需要临时员工多长时间？如果能找到至少持续一个月的工作就好了。

segments.speaker: AMBER
segments.en: So how long do people generally need temporary staff for?

segments.speaker: AMBER
segments.en: It would be great if I could get something lasting at least a month.

segments.speaker: WILLIAM
segments.en: That shouldn't be too difficult.
segments.zh: 那应该不太难。但你一开始更可能被提供一周的工作，然后可能会延长。很少会只派你去一两天。

segments.speaker: WILLIAM
segments.en: But you're more likely to be offered something for a week at first, which might get extended.

segments.speaker: WILLIAM
segments.en: It's unusual to be sent somewhere for just a day or two.

segments.speaker: AMBER
segments.en: Right.
segments.zh: 对。我听说薪水还不错——比在商店或餐馆工作好。

segments.speaker: AMBER
segments.en: I've heard the pay isn't too bad – better than working in a shop or a restaurant.

segments.speaker: WILLIAM
segments.en: Oh yes – definitely.
segments.zh: 哦，是的——绝对。时薪大约10英镑，运气好的话11英镑。

segments.speaker: WILLIAM
segments.en: The hourly rate is about £10, 11 if you're lucky.

segments.speaker: AMBER
segments.en: That's pretty good.
segments.zh: 那挺好的。我本来以为每小时只有八九英镑呢。

segments.speaker: AMBER
segments.en: I was only expecting to get eight or nine pounds an hour.

segments.speaker: WILLIAM
segments.en: Do you want me to tell you anything about the registration process?
segments.zh: 你想让我给你讲讲注册流程吗？

segments.speaker: AMBER
segments.en: Yes, please.
segments.zh: 好的，请讲。我知道得先面试。

segments.speaker: AMBER
segments.en: I know you have to have an interview.

segments.speaker: WILLIAM
segments.en: The interview usually takes about an hour and you should arrange that about a week in advance.
segments.zh: 面试通常要一个小时左右，你应该提前大约一周安排。

segments.speaker: AMBER
segments.en: I suppose I should dress smartly if it's for office work – I can probably borrow a suit from Mum.
segments.zh: 我想如果是办公室工作，我应该穿得正式点——也许可以跟妈妈借套西装。

segments.speaker: WILLIAM
segments.en: Good idea.
segments.zh: 好主意。穿得太正式总比太随意好。

segments.speaker: WILLIAM
segments.en: It's better to look too smart than too casual.

segments.speaker: AMBER
segments.en: Will I need to bring copies of my exam certificates or anything like that?
segments.zh: 我需要带考试证书复印件之类的东西吗？

segments.speaker: WILLIAM
segments.en: No – they don't need to see those, I don't think.
segments.zh: 不用——我觉得他们不需要看那些。

pairs.note: 题干问代理人名字，原文直接给出名字，完全一致。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问代理人名字，原文直接拼写名字 Jamieson，直接定位。

items.prompt: Best to call her in the

pairs.q: Best to call her in the
pairs.p: I wouldn't call her until the afternoon
pairs.note: 题干说最好在...打电话，原文说直到下午才打电话，意思相同。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问最佳打电话时间，原文说直到下午才打，所以答案是afternoon。

pairs.note: 题干中的'skills'在原文中直接出现，但原文否定了'business skills'和'computer systems'，强调'communication'才是真正重要的技能。
题干'必须拥有'对应原文'真正重要'，都是强调必要性。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句先否定其他技能，然后通过'it's communication that really matters'强调沟通能力是关键，与题干'good skills'对应。

items.prompt: Jobs are usually for at least one

pairs.note: 题干说至少一周，原文说一周，是具体化替换。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问工作通常至少多久，原文说一开始可能给一周的工作，所以答案就是week。

pairs.note: 题干中的Pay（报酬）被替换为原文中的hourly rate（时薪），意思相同。
题干中的£ per hour（每小时英镑）在原文中直接对应具体数字£10。

paraphrase.explain: 通过定位词'Pay'和'per hour'找到原文中的'hourly rate'和'£10'，从而确定答案。

pairs.note: 题干wear与原文dress同义，都表示‘穿’
原文直接出现a suit，与题干完全一致
题干interview对应原文office work，面试与办公室工作相关

paraphrase.explain: 通过定位词dress和a suit，在原文中找到‘borrow a suit’，从而确定答案suit。

items.prompt: Must bring your to the interview
They will ask questions about each applicant's
The you receive at interview will benefit you
Less is involved in applying for jobs
